parts.passageTitle: Air conditioning — History of an invention

passage.text:
Air conditioning — History of an invention
Willis Carrier designed the first air-conditioning unit in 1902, just a year after graduating from Cornell University with a Masters in Engineering. At a Brooklyn printing plant, fluctuations in heat and moisture were causing the size of the printing paper to keep changing slightly, making it hard to align different colours. Carrier's invention made it possible to control temperature and humidity levels and so align the colours. The invention also allowed industries such as film, processed food, textiles and pharmaceuticals to improve the quality of their products.
In 1914, the first air-conditioning device was installed in a private house. However, its size, similar to that of an early computer, meant it took up too much space to come into widespread use, and later models, such as the Weathermaker, which Carrier brought out in the 1920s, cost too much for most people. Cooling for human comfort, rather than industrial need, really took off when three air conditioners were installed in the J.L. Hudson Department Store in Detroit, Michigan. People crowded into the shop to experience the new invention. The fashion spread from department stores to cinemas, whose income rose steeply as a result of the comfort they provided.
To start with, money-conscious employers regarded air conditioning as a luxury. They considered that if they were paying people to work, they should not be paying for them to be comfortable as well. So in the 1940s and '50s, the industry started putting out a different message about its product: according to their research, installing air conditioning increased productivity amongst employees. They found that typists increased their output by 24% when transferred from a regular office to a cooled one.
However, air conditioning has its critics. Jed Brown, an environmentalist, complains that air conditioning is a factor in global warming. Unfortunately, he adds, because air conditioning leads to higher temperatures, people have to use it even more. However, he admits that it provides a healthier environment for many people in the heat of summer.

questions.prompt: When Willis Carrier invented air conditioning, his aim was to:
questions.explanation: EN: Carrier designed the first unit for a Brooklyn printing plant because heat/moisture caused paper sizes to fluctuate, making color alignment hard (solving factory problems). VI: Carrier đã thiết kế thiết bị đầu tiên cho một nhà in ở Brooklyn vì nhiệt độ/độ ẩm làm kích thước giấy thay đổi, gây khó khăn cho việc xếp màu (giải quyết các vấn đề trong nhà máy).

options.label: A. make workers feel cooler
B. produce more attractive paper
C. set up a new business
D. solve problems in a factory

questions.prompt: Home air conditioners were not popular at first because they were:
questions.explanation: EN: The text states: 'its size... meant it took up too much space to come into widespread use... cost too much for most people'. VI: Văn bản nêu rõ: 'kích thước của nó... đồng nghĩa với việc chiếm quá nhiều không gian để sử dụng rộng rãi... giá quá đắt với hầu hết mọi người'.

options.label: A. too big and expensive
B. not considered necessary.
C. too inefficient.
D. complicated to use.

questions.prompt: Employers refused to put air conditioning in workplaces at first because they:
questions.explanation: EN: 'money-conscious employers regarded air conditioning as a luxury. They considered that if they were paying people to work, they should not be paying for them to be comfortable as well'. VI: 'các chủ doanh nghiệp quan tâm đến tiền bạc coi điều hòa là thứ xa xỉ. Họ cho rằng nếu đã trả tiền để thuê người làm việc thì không nên trả thêm tiền để họ được thoải mái nữa'.

options.label: A. could not afford to pay for it.
B.  thought it was more suitable for cinemas.
C. did not want to spend money improving conditions
D.  thought people would not work so hard in comfortable conditions.

questions.prompt: What was the purpose of research done in the 1940s and '50s?
questions.explanation: EN: Because employers saw it as a luxury, 'the industry started putting out a different message... installing air conditioning increased productivity' to persuade businesses to buy it. VI: Vì các chủ doanh nghiệp coi đó là xa xỉ, 'ngành công nghiệp bắt đầu đưa ra một thông điệp khác... việc lắp điều hòa giúp tăng năng suất' để thuyết phục các doanh nghiệp mua hàng.

options.label: A. to make office workers produce more
B. to compare different types of air conditioner
C. to persuade businesses to buy air conditioners
D. to encourage employees to change offices

questions.prompt: What does Jed Brown say about air conditioning?
questions.explanation: EN: Brown notes air conditioning is a factor in global warming, but admits it 'provides a healthier environment for many people in the heat of summer'. VI: Brown lưu ý rằng điều hòa là tác nhân gây ấm lên toàn cầu, nhưng thừa nhận nó 'mang lại môi trường lành mạnh hơn cho nhiều người trong cái nóng mùa hè'.

options.label: A. In the future, everyone will need it.
B. Turning it off will not reduce global warming.
C. It can seriously damage people’s health.
D. It is good for people, but bad for the environment

parts.passageTitle: Rubik's Cube — How the puzzle achieved success

passage.text:
Rubik's Cube — How the puzzle achieved success
Erno Rubik first studied sculpture and then later architecture in Budapest, where he went on to become a teacher of interior design. It was while he was working as a teacher that he began the preliminary work on an invention that he called the 'Magic Cube'. Rubik was inspired by geometric puzzles such as the Chinese tangram, a puzzle consisting of various triangles, a square and a parallelogram which can be combined to create different shapes and figures. However, unlike the tangram, which is two-dimensional, Rubik was more interested in investigating how three-dimensional forms, such as the cube, could be moved and combined to produce other forms.
His design consisted of a cube made up of layers of individual smaller cubes, and each smaller cube could be turned in any direction except diagonally. To ensure that the cubes could move independently, without falling apart, Rubik first attempted to join them together using elastic bands. However, this proved to be impossible, so Rubik then solved the problem by assembling them using a rounded interior. This permitted them to move smoothly and easily. He experimented with different ways of marking the smaller cubes, but ended up with the simple solution of giving a different colour to each side. The object was to twist the layers of small cubes so that each side of the large cube was an identical colour.
Rubik took out a patent for the Cube in 1977 and started manufacturing it in the same year. The Cube came to the attention of a Hungarian businessman, Tibor Laczi, who then demonstrated it at the Nuremberg Toy Fair. When British toy expert Tom Kremer saw it, he thought it was amazing and he persuaded a manufacturer, Ideal Toys, to produce 1 million of them in 1979. Ideal Toys renamed the Cube after the toy's inventor, and in 1980, Rubik's Cube was shown at toy fairs all over the world. It won that year's prize in Germany for Best Puzzle. Rubik's Cube is believed to be the world's best-selling puzzle; since its invention, more than 300 million Cubes have been sold worldwide.

questionGroups.instruction:
Complete the summary below.
Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.

questions.prompt: Originally named the 1 ________, Rubik's Cube...
questions.explanation: EN: Rubik began work on an invention 'that he called the Magic Cube'. VI: Rubik bắt đầu nghiên cứu một phát minh 'mà ông gọi là Magic Cube'.

questions.prompt: consists of a number of smaller cubes organised in 2 ________.
questions.explanation: EN: 'made up of layers of individual smaller cubes'. VI: 'được cấu tạo từ các lớp hình lập phương nhỏ hơn riêng biệt'.

questions.prompt: The smaller cubes can be twisted in almost any way, though not 3 ________.
questions.explanation: EN: 'could be turned in any direction except diagonally'. VI: 'có thể xoay theo mọi hướng ngoại trừ xoay chéo'.

questions.prompt: The Cube's 4 ________ is shaped in a way that allows them to move smoothly.
questions.explanation: EN: 'assembling them using a rounded interior. This permitted them to move smoothly'. VI: 'lắp ghép chúng sử dụng một phần bên trong được bo tròn. Điều này cho phép chúng chuyển động mượt mà'.

questions.prompt: the aim is to organise the cubes so that the colours on the sides are 5 ________.
questions.explanation: EN: 'so that each side of the large cube was an identical colour'. VI: 'sao cho mỗi mặt của khối lập phương lớn có màu sắc đồng nhất'.

questions.prompt: The manufacturers changed the name to the name of its 6 ________.
questions.explanation: EN: 'renamed the Cube after the toy's inventor'. VI: 'đổi tên khối lập phương theo tên nhà phát minh của món đồ chơi'.

questions.prompt: It has now sold more than any other 7 ________ in the world.
questions.explanation: EN: 'world's best-selling puzzle; since its invention...'. VI: 'món đồ chơi xếp hình bán chạy nhất thế giới; kể từ khi phát minh...'

questionGroups.note:
Originally named the [1], Rubik’s Cube consists of a number of smaller cubes organised in [2]. The smaller cubes can be twisted in almost any way, though not [3]. The Cube’s [4] is shaped in a way that allows the smaller cubes to move smoothly. Each side of the smaller cubes has a different colour, and the aim of the puzzle is to organise the cubes so that the colours on the sides of the large cube are [5].
The manufacturers of the puzzle changed the name of the Cube to the name of its [6]. It has now sold more than any other [7] in the world.

questionGroups.instruction:
Match each statement with the correct option, A–F.
Write the correct letter in boxes 8–13 on your answer sheet.

questions.prompt: preliminary
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: preliminary.
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 1: "...he began the preliminary work on an invention that he called the “Magic Cube”."
Bước 3: Phân tích: Trong văn cảnh, Erno Rubik bắt đầu những công việc "sơ bộ, mở đầu" cho một phát minh mới. Điều này trùng khớp hoàn toàn với định nghĩa c: được làm hoặc xảy ra nhằm chuẩn bị cho sự kiện hoặc hoạt động chính (done or happening in order to prepare for the main event or activity).

questions.prompt: fall apart
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: falling apart.
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 3: "To ensure that the cubes could move independently, without falling apart, Rubik first..."
Bước 3: Phân tích: Tác giả nhắc đến việc giữ cho các khối lập phương nhỏ di chuyển độc lập mà không bị "rời ra, vỡ vụn". Định nghĩa này tương ứng với câu a: vỡ thành nhiều mảnh (break into pieces).

questions.prompt: attempt
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: attempted.
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 3: "...Rubik first attempted to join them together using elastic bands."
Bước 3: Phân tích: Rubik đã "cố gắng/thử" gắn kết các khối nhỏ lại với nhau bằng dây thun. Hành động này trùng khớp với định nghĩa f: cố gắng làm một điều gì đó, đặc biệt là việc khó khăn (try to do something, especially something difficult).

questions.prompt: assemble
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: assembling.
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 3: "...so Rubik then solved the problem by assembling them using a rounded interior."
Bước 3: Phân tích: Từ "assembling" ở đây chỉ việc "lắp ráp" các bộ phận lại với nhau để giải quyết vấn đề cấu trúc của khối lập phương. Nó khớp với định nghĩa b: chế tạo thứ gì đó bằng cách kết hợp các bộ phận lại với nhau (build something by joining parts together).

questions.prompt: experiment
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: experimented.
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 3: "He experimented with different ways of marking the smaller cubes..."
Bước 3: Phân tích: Rubik đã "thử nghiệm" nhiều cách khác nhau để đánh dấu các khối nhỏ nhằm tìm ra giải pháp. Hành động này tương ứng với định nghĩa e: thử làm một điều gì đó để khám phá xem nó như thế nào (try something in order to discover what it is like).

questions.prompt: object
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: object.
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 3: "The object was to twist the layers of small cubes so that each side of the large cube was an identical colour."
Bước 3: Phân tích: Từ "object" trong câu này mang ý nghĩa là "mục tiêu, mục đích" của trò chơi (xoay các lớp sao cho mỗi mặt có một màu đồng nhất). Do đó, nó đi kèm với định nghĩa d: mục đích của một cái gì đó (the purpose of something).

features.name: build something by joining parts together
done or happening in order to prepare for the main event or activity
try something in order to discover what it is like
try to do something, especially something difficult

parts.passageTitle: Marcel Bich — The man who made the ballpoint pen affordable

passage.text:
Marcel Bich — The man who made the ballpoint pen affordable
Marcel Bich, a French manufacturer of traditional ink pens, was the man who turned the ballpoint pen into an item that today almost anyone can afford. Bich was appalled at the poor quality of the ballpoint pens that were available, and was also shocked at their high cost. However, he recognised that the ballpoint was a firmly established invention, and he decided to design a cheap pen that worked well and would be commercially successful.
Bich went to the Biro brothers and asked them if he could use the design of their original invention in one of his own pens. In return, he offered to pay them every time he sold a pen. Then, for two years, Bich studied the detailed construction of every ballpoint pen that was being sold, often working with a microscope. By 1950, he was ready to introduce his new wonder: a plastic pen with a clear barrel that wrote smoothly, did not leak and only cost a few cents. He called it the 'Bic Cristal'.
Bich became extremely wealthy thanks to his invention, which had worldwide appeal. Over the next 60 years, his company, Societe Bic, opened factories all over the world and expanded its range of inexpensive products. Today, Bic is as famous for its lighters and razors as it is for its pens, and you can even buy a Bic mobile phone.

questionGroups.instruction:
Complete the summary below.
Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.

questions.prompt: It is thanks to Marcel Bich that most people today are able to 1 ________ a ballpoint pen.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

most people today

able to

ballpoint pen

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

Marcel Bich ... turned the ballpoint pen into an item that today almost anyone can afford.

Bước 3: Phân tích

EN:
The summary says "most people today are able to..."
The passage says "almost anyone can afford".

VI:
Bài tóm tắt diễn đạt lại:

almost anyone = most people today
can afford = có khả năng mua được

=> afford

questions.prompt: It was the bad quality and 2 ________ of available pens...
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

bad quality

available at the time

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

Bich was appalled at the poor quality of the ballpoint pens that were available, and was also shocked at their high cost.

Bước 3: Phân tích

EN:
The passage gives two problems:

poor quality
high cost

The summary already mentions bad quality, so we need the second problem.

VI:
Hai vấn đề của bút bi thời đó:

chất lượng kém
giá cao

=> high cost

questions.prompt: that inspired him to design a 3 ________ ballpoint pen...
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

design

ballpoint pen

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

he decided to design a cheap pen that worked well

Bước 3: Phân tích

EN:
cheap = inexpensive

VI:
Ông muốn thiết kế một cây bút rẻ tiền nhưng hoạt động tốt.

=> cheap

questions.prompt: After getting permission from the Biro brothers to base his pen on their 4 ________,
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

permission

Biro brothers

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

use the design of their original invention

Bước 3: Phân tích
EN:
The summary rewrites:
use the design of their original invention

base his pen on their original invention

VI:
Ông xin phép dùng thiết kế phát minh gốc của anh em nhà Biro.

=> original invention

questions.prompt: he carefully 5 ________ other ballpoints sold in the shops,
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

carefully

other ballpoints

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

for two years, Bich studied the detailed construction of every ballpoint pen

Bước 3: Phân tích

EN:
The summary changes:
studied the detailed construction
→ carefully studied

VI:
Ông nghiên cứu rất kỹ cấu tạo của các loại bút bi khác.

=> studied

questions.prompt: It was popular with the 6 ________, and Bich became very rich.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

popular

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

The public liked it immediately

Bước 3: Phân tích
EN:
liked immediately

popular with

VI:
Người dân thích nó ngay lập tức.

=> public

questions.prompt: His company, Bic, now makes a variety of cheap 7 ________, such as lighters and razors.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

lighters

razors

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

expanded its range of inexpensive products

Bước 3: Phân tích

EN:
lighters and razors are examples of products.

VI:
Bật lửa và dao cạo là các sản phẩm giá rẻ của Bic.

=> inexpensive products

questionGroups.note: It is thanks to Marcel Bich that most people today are able to [1] a ballpoint pen. It was the bad quality and [2] of the pens which were available at the time that inspired him to design a [3] ballpoint pen that would be both inexpensive and reliable. After getting permission from the Biro brothers to base his pen on their [4], he carefully [5] other ballpoints that were sold in the shops, and in 1950 introduced his own version, the ‘Bic Cristal’. It was popular with the [6], and Bich became very rich. His company, Bic, now makes a variety of cheap [7], such as lighters and razors.

parts.passageTitle: The ballpoint pen — How this popular item evolved

passage.text:
The ballpoint pen — How this popular item evolved
Most of us have at least one, but how did this popular item evolve?
One morning in 1945, a crowd of 5,000 people jammed the entrance of Gimbels Department Store in New York. The day before, Gimbels had placed a full-page advertisement in the New York Times for a wonderful new invention, the ballpoint pen. The advertisement described the pen as “fantastic” and “miraculous.” Although they were expensive, $12.50 each, all 10,000 pens in stock were sold on the first day.
In fact, this “new” pen was not new at all. In 1888, John Loud, a leather manufacturer, had invented a pen with a reservoir of ink and a rolling ball. However, his pen was never produced, and efforts by other people to produce a commercially successful one failed too. The main problem was with the ink. If it was too thin, the ink leaked out of the pen. If it was too thick, it didn’t come out of the pen at all.
Almost fifty years later, in 1935, a newspaper editor in Hungary thought he spent too much time filling his pens with ink. He decided to invent a better kind of pen. With the help of his brother, who was a chemist, he produced a ballpoint pen that didn’t leak when the pen wasn’t being used. The editor was called Ladislas Biro, and it was his name that people would associate more than any other with the ballpoint pen.
By chance, Biro met Augustine Justo, the Argentinian president. Justo was so impressed with Biro’s invention that he invited him to set up a factory in Argentina. In 1943, the first Biros were produced.
Unfortunately, they were not popular, since the pen needed to be held in a vertical position for the ink to come out. Biro redesigned the pen with a better ball, and in 1944 the new product was on sale throughout Argentina.
It was a North American, Milton Reynolds, who introduced the ballpoint pen to the USA. Copying Biro’s design, he produced the version that sold so well at Gimbels. Another American, Patrick Frawley, improved the design and in 1950 began producing a pen he called the Papermate. It was an immediate success, and within a few years, Papermates were selling in their millions around the world.

questionGroups.instruction:
Match each statement with the correct option, A–E.
Write the correct letter in boxes 1–5 on your answer sheet.

questions.prompt: John Loud
questions.explanation: EN: Loud 'invented a pen with a reservoir of ink and a rolling ball' in 1888. VI: Loud 'đã phát minh ra một cây bút có ngăn chứa mực và một viên bi xoay' vào năm 1888.

questions.prompt: Ladislas Biro
questions.explanation: EN: Biro was a newspaper editor who 'thought he spent too much time filling his pens with ink' and wanted to make his job easier. VI: Biro là một biên tập viên báo chí, người 'nghĩ rằng mình đã mất quá nhiều thời gian để bơm mực cho bút' và muốn công việc của mình dễ dàng hơn.

questions.prompt: Augustine Justo
questions.explanation: EN: Justo, the Argentinian president, 'invited him to set up a factory in Argentina'. VI: Justo, tổng thống Argentina, 'đã mời ông ấy thành lập một nhà máy ở Argentina'.

questions.prompt: Milton Reynolds
questions.explanation: EN: Reynolds was a North American who 'introduced the ballpoint pen to the USA, copying Biro's design'. VI: Reynolds là một người Bắc Mỹ 'đã đưa bút bi vào Mỹ, sao chép thiết kế của Biro'.

questions.prompt: Patrick Frawley
questions.explanation: EN: Frawley produced the Papermate which 'was an immediate success, and within a few years... selling in their millions around the world'. VI: Frawley sản xuất dòng bút Papermate và nó 'đã thành công ngay lập tức, và trong vòng vài năm... bán được hàng triệu chiếc trên khắp thế giới'.

questions.prompt: People went to Gimbels to buy a ballpoint pen because:
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
advertisement
fantastic
miraculous
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

The advertisement described the pen as "fantastic" and "miraculous".

Bước 3: Phân tích

EN: The advertisement told people the pen was wonderful.

VI: Quảng cáo nói cây bút rất tuyệt vời nên mọi người đổ xô đi mua.

=> B. they had been told how good the pens were.

options.label: A. they couldn't get them anywhere else
B. they had been told how good the pens were
C. they had never seen a ballpoint pen before
D. they thought the price was good

questions.prompt: Why were early ballpoint pens not produced commercially?
questions.explanation: EN: The main problem was the ink leaking or not coming out (they didn't work properly). VI: Vấn đề chính là mực bị rò rỉ hoặc không chảy ra (chúng hoạt động không đúng cách).

options.label: A. Nobody wanted to buy one
B. It cost too much to produce them
C. They used too much ink
D. They didn't work properly

questions.prompt: Why was Ladislas Biro's pen better than earlier models?
questions.explanation: EN: Biro produced a pen 'that didn't leak when the pen wasn't being used' (ink stayed in until needed). VI: Biro đã tạo ra một cây bút 'không bị rò rỉ mực khi không sử dụng' (mực được giữ lại cho đến khi cần).

options.label: A. It didn't need to be filled with ink as often
B. It was designed by a chemist
C. The ink stayed in the pen until it was needed
D. It was easier to use

questions.prompt: Biro's first commercially-produced pen:
questions.explanation: EN: They were not popular because 'the pen needed to be held in a vertical position for the ink to come out' (needed to be used in a certain way). VI: Chúng không phổ biến vì 'cây bút cần phải được giữ ở vị trí thẳng đứng thì mực mới chảy ra được' (chỉ hoạt động nếu dùng theo cách nhất định).

options.label: A. was produced in a factory owned by the Argentinian president
B. only worked if used in a certain way
C. was a major success
D. went on sale in 1944

questions.prompt: Patrick Frawley's pen:
questions.explanation: EN: Frawley 'improved the design' of the pen (a better version of an earlier model). VI: Frawley 'đã cải tiến thiết kế' của cây bút (một phiên bản tốt hơn của mẫu trước đó).

options.label: A. was a better version of an earlier model
B. took time to become successful
C. was the USA's first commercially successful ballpoint pen
D. was only successful in the USA
